moviesAndDocumentaries.title: GATTACA
moviesAndDocumentaries.description: A science-fiction drama about a society that ranks people by their genetic profiles.
moviesAndDocumentaries.genetics_relevance: Explores genetic selection, discrimination, identity, privacy, and the limits of genetic prediction.

moviesAndDocumentaries.title: Lorenzo's Oil
moviesAndDocumentaries.description: Parents of a child with adrenoleukodystrophy pursue an experimental treatment for his rare disorder.
moviesAndDocumentaries.genetics_relevance: Centers on a rare inherited disease, family advocacy, research, and the uncertainties of medical discovery.

moviesAndDocumentaries.title: The Gene: An Intimate History
moviesAndDocumentaries.description: A documentary series tracing the history of genetics through scientists, patients, and families.
moviesAndDocumentaries.genetics_relevance: Connects major discoveries, rare-disease research, gene editing, and the ethical questions raised by genomic medicine.

moviesAndDocumentaries.title: Human Nature
moviesAndDocumentaries.description: A documentary about CRISPR and the people shaping the future of gene editing.
moviesAndDocumentaries.genetics_relevance: Examines the scientific promise and ethical consequences of changing DNA, including possible treatments for genetic disease.

moviesAndDocumentaries.title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
moviesAndDocumentaries.description: A drama about Henrietta Lacks, her family, and the cells taken from her that transformed biomedical research.
moviesAndDocumentaries.genetics_relevance: Raises enduring questions about tissue research, consent, race, family, privacy, and genomic data.

dnaDetectiveStories.title: Golden State Killer and Genetic Genealogy
dnaDetectiveStories.description: Investigators used long-range familial searching and traditional police work to identify a suspect in a decades-old case.
dnaDetectiveStories.importance: A landmark example of forensic genetic genealogy and a prompt for continuing debate about DNA privacy and consent.

dnaDetectiveStories.title: The Boy in the Box Identified
dnaDetectiveStories.description: Philadelphia investigators used forensic genetic genealogy to identify a child known for decades only as the Boy in the Box.
dnaDetectiveStories.importance: Shows how DNA, records, and genealogy can restore a name to an unidentified person after many decades.

dnaDetectiveStories.title: DNA Doe Project Case Stories
dnaDetectiveStories.description: A nonprofit initiative applies investigative genetic genealogy to unidentified-person cases across North America.
dnaDetectiveStories.importance: Its case work demonstrates how DNA matches and family-tree research can return names to unknown individuals.

dnaDetectiveStories.title: Diagnosis and Discovery: NIH Undiagnosed Diseases Program
dnaDetectiveStories.description: Six medical mysteries illustrate how careful clinical work and genome sequencing can lead families toward answers.
dnaDetectiveStories.importance: Highlights collaborative diagnosis and the value of sequencing for people whose rare conditions have resisted routine testing.

dnaMusic.title: Music from the Genome: Allele
dnaMusic.description: DNA analysis from 40 singers helped shape individual parts in a choral composition.
dnaMusic.genetics_relevance: Turns genetic variation into a shared musical experience while reflecting both individuality and connection.

dnaMusic.title: Protein Music
dnaMusic.description: A pioneering approach assigns musical notes to DNA bases and uses genetic sequences as the foundation for compositions.
dnaMusic.genetics_relevance: Demonstrates an early method of translating biological sequence patterns into sound.

dnaMusic.title: Your DNA Song
dnaMusic.description: A participatory work in which a listener's genetic information is transformed into a personalized choral phrase.
dnaMusic.genetics_relevance: Makes genetic difference audible by mapping selected DNA variations into music.

dnaArts.title: The Ladder
dnaArts.artist: Mary Ellen Scherl / National Institutes of Health
dnaArts.description: A public sculpture whose double-helical ladder and four child figures celebrate DNA and human diversity.
dnaArts.genetics_relevance: Uses the familiar structure and four chemical letters of DNA to invite public curiosity about genomics.

dnaArts.title: Intersecting Data Fields
dnaArts.artist: Jennifer Parker, Karolina Karlic, and the UCSC Genomics Institute
dnaArts.description: A collaborative portrait exhibition exploring the people, objects, and stories behind genomics research.
dnaArts.genetics_relevance: Uses socially engaged art to communicate the diversity, practice, and human impact of genomics.

dnaArts.title: Ferocious Beauty: Genome
dnaArts.description: A performance project created with genetic scientists to explore inheritance, identity, and contemporary genomics.
dnaArts.genetics_relevance: Brings genetics into dance and public dialogue through collaboration between artists and researchers.

dnaArts.title: DNA Origami
dnaArts.description: A printable art-and-science activity for building a three-dimensional paper model of DNA.
dnaArts.genetics_relevance: Makes base pairing and the twists of the double helix tangible through hands-on visual design.

picks.reason_selected: A widely recognized starting point for conversations about genetic discrimination and human potential.
A recent, hopeful look at how genomics and teamwork can resolve difficult medical mysteries.
A memorable example of real genetic variation becoming a communal work of music.
A welcoming public artwork that connects DNA, identity, family, and discovery.
